message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): add inject_pane_with_cx + close inject_with_cx seam (tick 295)

Adds a cx-first sibling of `ScrollbackInjector::inject_pane` and routes
`inject_with_cx` through it, closing a maybe-cx seam hole in the
scrollback injection path.

**New cx-first sibling `inject_pane_with_cx` (restore_scrollback.rs L421):**

- Mirrors `inject_pane` chunk-by-chunk injection logic
- Adds `cx.checkpoint()` before each chunk send so cancel lands between
  mux RPCs, not after completing the full injection
- Routes chunk send through `send_text_with_cx(cx, new_pane_id, chunk)`
- Routes inter-chunk delay through `sleep_with_cx(cx, delay)`

**`inject_with_cx` updated (L344):**

- `self.inject_pane(*old_id, new_id, scrollback)` →
  `self.inject_pane_with_cx(cx, *old_id, new_id, scrollback)`

Before this tick, `inject_with_cx` threaded cx through the outer
per-pane iteration (checkpoint between panes at L327) but dropped cx
at the per-chunk boundary because `inject_pane` was ambient. A
large-scrollback injection (e.g., 500kB per pane, 100ms inter-chunk)
could run for minutes before cancel could take effect.

Now the full injection chain is cx-aware end-to-end:
  per-pane loop (checkpoint) → per-chunk loop (checkpoint +
  send_text_with_cx + sleep_with_cx)

Legacy ambient `inject_pane` (L362) is retained for the ambient
`inject` caller at L211 (legacy path preserved).

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 109 (187-295).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 55 (prior 54 + restore_scrollback 1
  — tracks the one added cx-first sibling method; the call-site update
  to route through it is implicit).
main.rs cross-crate total: 192 sites (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
